page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range. Notably, the PassMark memory score is in the top 10 percent, reflecting highly efficient memory subsystem performance. Cryptographic and integer operations are accelerated via hardware flags such as AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and BMI2.

This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching layers like Redis, and medium-scale web serving. While it demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale model prompt processing due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. The high memory density relative to compute resources offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-bound applications that do not require massive parallel processing power.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 8 physical cores and hyper-threading, it provides dedicated CPU resources with a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25 percent and a top-tier PassMark memory score in the top 10 percent, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Hardware-assisted cryptography is supported via AES and SHA_NI flags. This configuration is optimized for memory-bound workloads such as database operations, in-memory caching, and web serving, though it is limited in large-scale AI inference tasks due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core and top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 features 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading
Top 10 percent PassMark memory score and top 25 percent single-core CPU performance
Hardware-accelerated cryptography with AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Best suited for memory-bound databases, Redis caching, and web serving
